title: [A1] Structured diagnostics with source spans
motivation: Today, parser/evaluator errors are plain strings without spans. This makes IDE integration impossible. The CONCEPTS-COMPARISION and FEATURE-COMPARISION docs flag diagnostics as a parity gap against Lean/Rocq/Isabelle.
goal: Every error returned by parser, evaluator, and (later) type checker is a structured `Diagnostic { code, message, span: { file, line, col } }`.
api:
- JS: `evaluate(code: string): { results: number[]; diagnostics: Diagnostic[] }`.
- Rust: `pub fn evaluate(src: &str) -> EvaluateResult { results: Vec<f64>, diagnostics: Vec<Diagnostic> }`.
criteria: Every existing error path returns a `Diagnostic` instead of throwing.
Each `Diagnostic` carries an error code (e.g. `E001` for unknown symbol) listed in `docs/DIAGNOSTICS.md`.
CLI prints diagnostics with file:line:col and a caret (`^`) under the offending token.
Tests added in both JS and Rust (or split tracked in a parity issue).
outOfScope: Quick-fix code actions; multi-file diagnostic aggregation (those belong with the LSP issue).

title: [A2] Interactive REPL
motivation: Comparison doc lists REPL as `Part` for RML. Lean/Rocq/Isabelle have first-class REPLs; users expect one for any modern proving system.
goal: A REPL that maintains a persistent `Env` between user inputs and prints diagnostics inline.
lino:
```
rml> (a: a is a)
rml> ((a = a) has probability 1)
rml> (? (a = a))
1
```
criteria: Tests cover state preservation and meta-commands.

title: [A3] Trace mode for evaluation
motivation: Without a step-by-step trace it is impossible to teach the engine, debug operator-redefinition pitfalls, or build a derivation tree later.
goal: `--trace` flag prints every reduction step, operator resolution, and assignment lookup with span info.
lino:
```
$ rml --trace demo.lino
[span demo.lino:3:1] resolve (and: avg)
[span demo.lino:5:1] eval (a = a) → assigned 1
```
api: CLI flag `--trace` in both `js/` and `rust/` runners; library-level option `trace: true`.
criteria: Tests verify trace lines for representative programs.
outOfScope: Time-travel debugging or step-back; trace-driven proof export (planned for phase C).

title: [A4] Document operator-redefinition design rationale
motivation: Newcomers often expect classical fixed semantics. Without a written rationale for runtime operator redefinition, users will file bugs about behaviour that is by design.
goal: Add `docs/CONFIGURABILITY.md` capturing why every operator is redefinable, the precedence rules at runtime, and how this differs from Lean/Rocq fixed semantics.
criteria: Explains range, valence, operator, aggregator, and truth-constant configuration.
outOfScope: Full philosophy of paradox tolerance (covered by H4 / a separate `docs/PHILOSOPHY.md`).

title: [A5] English-readability lint for `lib/` and `examples/`
motivation: The plan's design promise is that every link reads as an English sentence. Without a lint, drift creeps back in.
goal: A small lint script that flags links violating the naming conventions: operator-only links without word-form alternatives, identifiers without hyphens, etc.
criteria: CI fails if `examples/` or `lib/` (when introduced) contain violations.
outOfScope: A full English NLP analysis; the lint is heuristic-based.

title: [B1] File imports with cycle detection
motivation: Comparison doc rows "Module/import system" and "Namespaces" are `Part`/`No` for RML. Required to scale beyond single-file demos and to support libraries (phase G) and self-bootstrap (phase J).
goal: Add `(import "<relative-path>.lino")` that loads another file's declarations into the current env. Cycles produce a structured diagnostic.
criteria: Caching: each file is loaded once.
Tests cover linear chains, diamonds, and cycles.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [B2] Namespaces and qualified references
motivation: Without namespaces, library names collide. Lean, Rocq, Isabelle all qualify with `Module.symbol` or analogue.
goal: A file can declare `(namespace classical)` and exported names become `classical.true`, `classical.and`, etc. Imports can be aliased via `(import "lib/classical.lino" as cl)`.
lino:
```
(namespace classical)
(and: min)
(or: max)
```
imported as:
```
(import "classical.lino" as cl)
(? (cl.and 1 0))
```
api: Resolver consults active namespace + alias table.
criteria: Re-binding a name in the importing file shadows the imported one with a warning diagnostic.
Tests for shadowing, qualified lookup, alias collisions.
outOfScope: Open/close namespace blocks within a single file (deferred).

title: [C1] Proof-producing evaluator
motivation: Concept rows "Machine-checked proof terms" and "Proof-producing evaluator" are `No`/`Part`. The trusted-kernel story starts here: every truth value should come with an auditable derivation.
goal: Each query optionally returns a derivation tree consisting of links: `(by <rule> <subderivations>)`.
api:
- JS: `evaluate(src, { withProofs: true })` returns `{ results, proofs }`.
- Rust: `Evaluator::with_proofs(true)`.
criteria: Derivations are themselves links and can be printed as `.lino`.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.
outOfScope: Proof compression; proof terms for probabilistic operators beyond a single sample (deferred).

title: [C2] Independent proof-replay checker
motivation: A proof-producing evaluator is only valuable if a small, separately auditable program can replay the proof. This is the trusted-kernel cornerstone.
goal: A separate executable `rml-check` consumes a `.lino` file containing both the program and its derivation tree and verifies that the derivation produces the claimed truth value.
lino:
```
$ rml-check program.lino proofs.lino
OK: 7 derivations replayed.
```
criteria: Checker depends only on the kernel (no evaluator).
Tests verify that mutated proof trees fail.
Checker's source is small (target: ≤ 500 LOC) so it can be audited.

title: [C3] Metatheorem checker over encoded systems
motivation: A universal formal-system constructor must be able to check metatheorems about user-defined systems (e.g. `plus is total on Natural`). This is the Twelf-style guarantee.
goal: Given a relation defined as a family of links plus a totality declaration (D12) and modes (D15), check that the relation is a total function on its declared domain.
lino:
```
(relation plus
  (plus zero (Natural n) n)
  (plus (succ m) (Natural n) (succ (plus m n))))
(mode plus +input +input -output)
(total plus)
```
criteria: Pass/fail diagnostic with counter-witness on failure.
Tests cover plus, less-than-or-equal, append on lists.
outOfScope: Proof of totality for non-structurally-decreasing definitions; that is delegated to D13.

title: [C5] Soundness statement
motivation: Users need a written statement of what the trusted kernel guarantees and the chosen aggregator family for which soundness is claimed.
goal: Add `docs/SOUNDNESS.md` explaining what the trusted kernel guarantees and which operators are part of the trusted base. (Note: in a paradox-tolerant logic, soundness is stated relative to a chosen aggregator family.)
criteria: Doc reviewed by maintainer; linked from README.

title: [D1] Promote dependent types and Pi to a documented kernel
motivation: The dependent-type prototype is undocumented. Without typing rules in writing, no one can audit or extend the typed kernel.
goal: Specify the typing rules for `(Pi …)`, `(lambda …)`, `apply`, type membership `(of)` in `docs/KERNEL.md`.
lino:
```
(succ: (Pi (Natural n) Natural))
(identity: lambda (Natural x) x)
(apply identity zero)
```
api: Typing rules realised in evaluator/type-checker code paths.
criteria: `docs/KERNEL.md` exists with a rule per construct.
Existing examples checked against the spec.
outOfScope: Inductive families (D10), polymorphism (D9), universes beyond two levels (D5).

title: [D2] Beta reduction in the evaluator
motivation: Without β-reduction, the type kernel cannot evaluate applied lambdas, blocking everything downstream.
goal: `(apply (lambda (Natural x) (x + 1)) 0)` reduces to `1` via β.
criteria: β-reduction tests for closed and open terms.
outOfScope: η, ζ, ι reductions (covered separately in D3/D4 as appropriate).

title: [D3] Definitional equality / convertibility
motivation: Type checking requires deciding when two terms are equal up to reduction. Without it, we cannot match a user's term against a declared type.
goal: An equality decision procedure that handles β, η (optional), and assignment lookup.
criteria: Test cases from the comparison-doc "Definitional equality" row pass.
η-conversion behind a feature flag if it complicates the kernel.

title: [D4] Full normalization for typed fragment
motivation: Some queries demand a normal form (e.g. comparing canonical proofs).
goal: Implement weak-head normal form and full normal form for typed link terms.
criteria: Termination proven for the typed fragment by D13.

title: [D5] Universe hierarchy as a checked layer
motivation: Avoiding paradoxes in a typed core requires universe stratification (or controlled circularity). RML chooses configurable strictness.
goal: `(Type 0)`, `(Type 1)`, … become checked levels; `((Type 0) of (Type 1))` returns 1, `((Type 1) of (Type 0))` returns 0.
criteria: Coexistence with self-referential `(Type: Type Type)` documented and tested.
outOfScope: Universe polymorphism (deferred to a future issue).

title: [D6] Bidirectional type checker
motivation: A bidirectional checker gives clear, local error messages and is the standard for dependent types.
goal: A type checker with check/synthesize modes; produces structured diagnostics on failure.
lino:
```
((zero: Natural zero) check)
((apply succ zero) synth) → Natural
```

title: [D7] Higher-order abstract syntax (HOAS) helpers
motivation: Encoding object-language binders without a HOAS pattern leads to fragile substitution code.
goal: Provide a documented HOAS encoding pattern (binders represented as `lambda` of the host) plus convenience helpers for common cases (e.g. `(forall (Natural x) body)` desugaring to `(Pi (Natural x) body)`).
lino:
```
(forall (Natural x) ((x + 0) = x))
; desugars to
(Pi (Natural x) ((x + 0) = x))
```
criteria: Lambda-calculus example uses HOAS and round-trips.

title: [D8] Capture-avoiding substitution and freshness
motivation: β reduction, induction principles, and self-encoded evaluators all need correct substitution.
goal: Kernel primitive for substitution; `nabla`-style freshness quantifier `(fresh x in body)`.
criteria: Substitution tests for all binder forms.
`(fresh x in body)` rejected when `x` already appears in the context.

title: [D9] Prenex polymorphism
motivation: Many basic library functions (identity, compose, apply) require polymorphism.
goal: Allow `(forall A (Pi (A x) A))` style polymorphic types in declarations.
lino:
```
(identity: forall A (Pi (A x) A))
(identity: lambda (Type A) (lambda (A x) x))
```
api: Type checker handles forall in prenex position.
criteria: Polymorphic identity, polymorphic apply, polymorphic compose all type-check.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [D10] Inductive families with eliminators
motivation: Lean/Rocq/Agda all rely on inductive types for libraries. RML must offer this without losing operator-redefinition semantics.
goal: First-class inductive declarations encoded as link signatures plus a generated eliminator.
lino:
```
(inductive Natural
  (constructor zero)
  (constructor (succ (Pi (Natural n) Natural))))
```
generates:
```
(Natural-rec
  (lambda (Type C)
    (lambda (C base)
      (lambda (Pi (Natural n) (Pi (C ih) C)) step)
      (lambda (Natural target) ...))))
```
criteria: Lists, vectors, and propositional equality definable.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [D11] Coinductive families and productivity
motivation: Streams, infinite trees, and many fixed-point examples need coinduction.
goal: `coinductive` declarations plus a productivity check based on guarded corecursion.
api: Productivity checker invoked alongside type checker.

title: [D12] Totality checking (Twelf-style)
motivation: A metatheorem checker (C3) needs to verify totality. Twelf demonstrates how to do this via modes and structural decrease.
goal: Given a relation with declared modes, decide whether it is total.
criteria: Plus, less-than-or-equal, append on lists checked total.

title: [D13] Termination checking
motivation: Productive non-termination is allowed in RML's evaluator, but the typed fragment needs decidable termination.
goal: Recursive definitions must reduce a structural-order argument; decreasing-order checks.
lino:
```
(define plus
  (case (zero n) n)
  (case ((succ m) n) (succ (plus m n))))
; structural decrease on first argument
```
criteria: Ackermann rejected without explicit measure; with a measure, accepted.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [D14] Coverage checking for case-style relations
motivation: Missing cases in pattern definitions cause silent failures. Coverage checks turn these into diagnostics.
goal: Every input pattern that the relation can match must be covered.
api: Coverage checker integrated into type checker.
criteria: Missing case produces a structured diagnostic with example.

title: [D15] Mode declarations
motivation: Totality and coverage need to know which arguments are inputs vs outputs.
goal: Each relation argument is `+input`, `-output`, or `*either`; checked by D12 and D14.
criteria: Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [D16] World declarations
motivation: Twelf's `%worlds` declaration constrains which constants may appear free in arguments — useful for mechanised metatheory.
goal: Twelf `%worlds` analogue for declaring which constants may appear free in a relation's arguments.
api: Optional — gated behind a feature flag if it complicates the kernel.
criteria: Optional — gated behind a feature flag.

title: [E1] Tactic language as links
motivation: Lean/Rocq/Isabelle have rich tactic languages. RML must offer composable proof steps without breaking the "everything is a link" invariant.
goal: Tactics are links interpreted by a tactic engine; they transform a proof state. Examples: `(by reflexivity)`, `(by symmetry)`, `(by transitivity <intermediate>)`, `(suppose <hypothesis>)`, `(introduce <variable>)`.
lino:
```
(theorem plus-zero (Pi (Natural n) ((plus n zero) = n))
  (proof
    (induction n
      (case zero (by reflexivity))
      (case (succ m) (rewrite (induction-hypothesis m) in goal)))))
```
api: `runTactics(state, tactics)` returns updated state and diagnostics.
criteria: Built-in tactics: reflexivity, symmetry, transitivity, induction, suppose, introduce, by, rewrite, exact.
Failed tactic produces structured diagnostic with current goal printed.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.
outOfScope: Custom user tactics (deferred to a follow-up).

title: [E2] Simplifier and rewriting
motivation: Simplification is the most-used tactic in real proofs. RML needs a configurable rewrite engine.
goal: `(simplify in <goal>)` applies a configured rewrite set; `(rewrite <eq> in <goal>)` uses a single equation; congruence handled by walking the link tree.
criteria: Tests cover direction of rewriting, occurrence selection, and termination guard.

title: [E3] Bounded proof search
motivation: Many proofs are mostly mechanical. A bounded backwards searcher closes goals automatically.
goal: `(by search depth N)` performs depth-bounded backwards search using available lemmas.
criteria: Search returns a derivation tree, not just a yes/no.

title: [E4] Counter-model finder for finite valences
motivation: Showing a formula is non-tautological is as valuable as proving it. For finite valences (3-valued, Belnap), brute force suffices.
goal: For finite-valence configurations, exhaustively search valuations to find a falsifying witness.
criteria: Demonstrates that excluded middle is non-tautological in 3-valued Kleene.

title: [E5] Macro / template mechanism for reusable link shapes
motivation: Library authors want named patterns: `(commutative-and a b)` should expand into a known theorem template.
goal: A `template` declaration that names a link shape with placeholders; uses are expanded before evaluation.
lino:
```
(template (commutative-and a b)
  (theorem (and a b) = (and b a)))
```
api: Pre-evaluation expander invoked by the parser/evaluator pipeline.

title: [F1] Lean 4 export of RML fragments
motivation: External certification: a Lean-checkable artefact strengthens trust and connects RML to mathlib.
goal: Translate a typed link fragment (no probabilistic operators) into Lean 4 source.
criteria: Round-trip test on a set of examples.

title: [F2] Rocq export
motivation: Same as F1 for Rocq — interoperability with the largest classical-logic ecosystem.
goal: Translate a typed link fragment (no probabilistic operators) into Rocq source.
criteria: Round-trip test on a set of examples.

title: [F3] Isabelle export
motivation: Same as F1 for Isabelle/Pure or Isabelle/HOL — proof-pearl exchange.
goal: Translate a typed link fragment (no probabilistic operators) into Isabelle source.
criteria: Round-trip test on a set of examples.

title: [F4] Pecan-style automatic-sequence backend (optional)
motivation: Demonstrates that domain-specific decision procedures plug into RML cleanly.
goal: A plugin that ships decisions about Buchi automata when the user declares the problem inside a `(domain automatic-sequences)` block.
criteria: At least one classic example (e.g. Thue-Morse word) decided.

title: [F5] SMT-LIB bridge
motivation: Real proofs benefit from SMT solvers (Z3, CVC5). The trusted-kernel design accepts external decisions as `(by smt-trusted <solver>)` nodes.
goal: `(by smt)` tactic dispatches to a configured SMT solver; the result is wrapped as a derivation node `(by smt-trusted <solver>)`.
api: Configurable solver path; tactic invokes solver via SMT-LIB.
outOfScope: Replaying solver proofs (use proof-format-specific bridges instead).

title: [F6] TPTP bridge for first-order ATPs
motivation: First-order ATPs (E, Vampire, SPASS) use TPTP. The bridge enables `(by atp)`.
goal: Same idea as F5 for TPTP-format ATPs.
api: Configurable ATP path; tactic emits TPTP and parses results.

title: [F7] Program extraction to JS / Rust
motivation: Verified algorithms become more useful when extracted to mainstream languages.
goal: Typed link programs without probabilistic operators extract to clean JS or Rust source.
criteria: Extracted code passes its own tests.
outOfScope: Extraction to other targets (Haskell, OCaml) — deferred.

title: [G1] Standard library: classical logic (`lib/classical/`)
motivation: A traditional starting point. Demonstrates that RML can host vanilla classical logic alongside its probabilistic and Belnap defaults.
goal: Boolean operators, classical ND rules, excluded middle, double negation, De Morgan packaged under `lib/classical/` with a `(namespace classical)` line.
criteria: Library file has a header comment with English summary.
Test file in `js/test/lib-classical.test.mjs` and Rust equivalent.
outOfScope: Linear logic, intuitionistic logic (consider follow-up libraries).

title: [G2] Standard library: first-order logic (`lib/first-order/`)
motivation: Quantifiers are the bread and butter of mathematics; RML must offer English-readable forms.
goal: Quantifiers using `(forall (Type X) body)` / `(exists …)` packaged under `lib/first-order/`.

title: [G3] Standard library: higher-order logic (`lib/higher-order/`)
motivation: HOL-style quantification over predicates is required by many libraries.
goal: HOL-style quantification over predicates packaged under `lib/higher-order/`.
lino:
```
(forall ((Pi (Natural n) Boolean) P) ((P zero) implies (forall (Natural n) (P (succ n)))))
```

title: [G4] Standard library: modal logic (`lib/modal/`)
motivation: K, T, S4, S5 with Kripke-frame interpretations are foundational and demonstrate RML's configurability.
goal: K, T, S4, S5 axioms; Kripke-frame interpretation packaged under `lib/modal/`.

title: [G5] Standard library: provability logic (`lib/provability/`)
motivation: GL and interpretability fragments showcase RML's metatheoretic muscle.
goal: GL axioms; interpretability fragment packaged under `lib/provability/`.

title: [G6] Standard library: set theory (`lib/set-theory/`)
motivation: Extensionality, pairing, union, separation, replacement, infinity — the classical foundation.
goal: Extensionality, pairing, union, separation, replacement, infinity packaged under `lib/set-theory/`.

title: [G7] Standard library: arithmetic (`lib/arithmetic/`)
motivation: Peano + decimal-precision lemmas anchor every other library.
goal: Peano axioms, decimal-precision lemmas, `<`, `≤` packaged under `lib/arithmetic/`.

title: [G8] Standard library: algebra (`lib/algebra/`)
motivation: Magma → group → ring is the canonical algebra path; long-term, may be deferred.
goal: Magma, monoid, group, ring packaged under `lib/algebra/` (long-term, may be deferred).

title: [G9] Standard library: programming-language theory (`lib/programming-language/`)
motivation: Untyped λ-calculus, STLC, type safety — the textbook syllabus, demonstrating RML's expressive power.
goal: Untyped λ-calculus, STLC, type-safety theorem packaged under `lib/programming-language/`.

title: [G10] Standard library: probabilistic / Belnap (`lib/probabilistic/`)
motivation: RML's most distinctive territory. A library of probabilistic and Belnap-bilattice helpers makes this superpower easy to use.
goal: Bayesian-network helpers, fuzzy-control example, Belnap-bilattice helpers, paradox catalogue packaged under `lib/probabilistic/`.

title: [H1] Language Server Protocol implementation
motivation: Modern provers ship LSPs. Without one, RML cannot meet user expectations for editor integration.
goal: An LSP server that wraps the JS or Rust evaluator and exposes diagnostics, hover documentation, go-to-definition, and (optional) completion.
api: Stdio-based LSP server invoked from editors.
criteria: LSP server passes a smoke-test suite.
outOfScope: Editor-specific UI (delegated to H2 for VS Code).

title: [H2] VS Code extension
motivation: VS Code is the most widely used editor in the formal-methods community.
goal: A VS Code extension that consumes the LSP from H1 and adds syntax highlighting plus a `.lino` language config.

title: [H3] Generated API/reference docs
motivation: Reference documentation is mandatory for adoption.
goal: JSDoc and rustdoc generation; published to GitHub Pages on release.
api: CI job builds and publishes docs.

title: [H4] Backward-compatibility policy + release process
motivation: A stable evolution policy lowers the cost of adoption for downstream users.
goal: `docs/COMPATIBILITY.md` describing semver application to JS and Rust crates, deprecation procedure, and release cadence.
criteria: Doc reviewed by maintainer; linked from README.

title: [H5] Literate `.lino` format
motivation: Literate proofs/exposition are the lingua franca of mathematical software.
goal: Allow `.lino.md` files where prose and code blocks alternate; tooling extracts code blocks for evaluation.
criteria: Extractor works on a representative sample of literate files.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [H6] Online wasm playground
motivation: Frictionless online experimentation drives adoption.
goal: A small static site that runs the JS or wasm-compiled Rust evaluator in the browser; supports examples and shareable URLs.

title: [H7] Tutorials and walkthroughs
motivation: Newcomer onboarding requires progressively richer tutorials.
goal: `docs/tutorials/` with progressively richer walkthroughs: classical → fuzzy → probabilistic → typed → metatheory → self-bootstrap.
criteria: At least four tutorials (classical, probabilistic, typed, self-bootstrap).

title: [H8] Docker support
motivation: Reproducible environments — useful for CI and contributors.
goal: `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` for both implementations.

title: [I1] Shared test corpus
motivation: Without a shared corpus, JS and Rust drift apart.
goal: Move the test corpus into a shared `test-corpus/` folder consumed by both JS and Rust suites.
criteria: Suites in both implementations consume the same corpus.
Tests added in both JS and Rust.

title: [I2] Parity CI job
motivation: A CI gate prevents regressions across implementations.
goal: A CI job that runs every `test-corpus/*.lino` through both JS and Rust and asserts identical output.

title: [J1] Encode the LiNo grammar as links
motivation: Self-bootstrap requires that the grammar of the language is itself describable in the language.
goal: A `lib/self/grammar.lino` describing the LiNo grammar as link rules. The grammar is interpretable by an in-RML parser.
criteria: Produces the same AST as the host parser (modulo presentation).

title: [J2] Encode the evaluator as links
motivation: An encoded evaluator demonstrates that RML is meta-logic capable of reasoning about itself.
goal: `lib/self/evaluator.lino` describing the evaluation rules of every built-in operator. Reads as a list of `(rule …)` links.
criteria: Encoded evaluator reproduces host outputs on the example corpus.

title: [J3] Encode the type layer as links
motivation: The encoded RML must include the typing rules to be a faithful reflection.
goal: `lib/self/types.lino` describing the typing rules.
criteria: Encoded type checker accepts/rejects the same programs as the host.

title: [J4] Encode operators and aggregators as links
motivation: Operators are the most-used part of RML; encoding them as links proves operator semantics are first-class data.
goal: `lib/self/operators.lino` containing a relation per operator (avg, min, max, product, probabilistic_sum, decimal arithmetic).
criteria: Encoded operator outputs match host outputs to 12 decimal places.

title: [J5] Encode the metatheorem checker as links
motivation: Encoding the metatheorem checker closes the loop: the system can describe how it itself is checked.
goal: `lib/self/metatheorem.lino` describing the totality, termination, coverage, mode, and world checks (subject to availability — D12–D16).
criteria: Encoded checker passes on the host's metatheorem test set.

title: [J6] Bootstrap test: encoded RML evaluates the example corpus
motivation: The bootstrap test is the moment of truth — RML evaluating itself on real examples.
goal: A CI job runs every `test-corpus/*.lino` through the encoded RML (driven by the host RML) and asserts identical output to the host RML.

title: [J7] Tutorial: "RML in RML"
motivation: The capstone is meaningless without a narrative readers can follow.
goal: `docs/tutorials/self-bootstrap.md` walking through the encoded files in narrative order, suitable for a beginner.

title: [J-EPIC] Universal formal-system constructor: feature-parity audit
motivation: The original issue (#26) calls for a universal formal-system constructor that surpasses traditional provers in configurability while reaching parity in feature richness, capped by self-reimplementation. This epic tracks completion against the comparison-doc rows and links every other planned issue.
goal: A tracking issue that references every other phase issue and tracks completion against the comparison-doc rows.
criteria: All phase-A through phase-J issues linked from this epic.
Comparison-doc rows mapped to filed issues.
Closed when J6 and J7 are complete.
